question: What is ARXML, and why is it more than an XML file format?
answer: ARXML is the standardized serialization of AUTOSAR model elements.
Its schemas encode relationships, references, packages and release-specific semantics.
Correct exchange requires model consistency, namespace/schema compatibility and ownership rules.
Manual text editing is risky because semantic validation matters more than XML syntax.
probes: Can two valid ARXML files still be incompatible?
How do UUIDs and references affect merging?

question: Explain the major AUTOSAR artifacts exchanged between OEM, Tier-1, application and BSW teams.
answer: System Description captures vehicle-level components, communication and deployment information.
ECU Extract provides the subset relevant to an ECU.
SWC descriptions define component contracts and internal behavior metadata.
BSW module descriptions define module capabilities and configuration parameters.
ECU configuration values and generated artifacts complete the ECU realization.
probes: Who should own the communication matrix?
Which artifact is the supplier contract?

question: Differentiate System Description, ECU Extract, ECU Configuration Values and generated code.
answer: System Description is vehicle/system-level design intent.
ECU Extract is a filtered ECU-specific view, not the complete ECU configuration.
ECUC values configure BSW and related ECU parameters.
Generators transform validated models into RTE/BSW code and configuration artifacts.
Each stage needs traceable baselines and compatibility control.
probes: Can ECUC be regenerated from an ECU Extract alone?
What is handwritten after generation?

question: What is the purpose of RTE contract-phase and generation-phase activities?
answer: Contract phase provides SWC-visible APIs and headers based on the component contract before final ECU mapping is complete.
It enables application development and compilation against stable interfaces.
Generation phase uses ECU allocation, communication and OS mappings to produce the concrete RTE realization.
Interface changes between phases must be controlled and revalidated.
probes: Can contract headers be used as final RTE?
What changes when a port becomes inter-ECU?

question: Explain pre-compile, link-time and post-build configuration classes and their trade-offs.
answer: Pre-compile configuration is fixed during compilation and enables optimization.
Link-time configuration is selected when linking and offers limited late variation.
Post-build configuration allows data changes after code build within defined compatibility limits.
Later binding increases variant flexibility but raises validation, integrity and compatibility concerns.
Safety-relevant post-build data needs identification and controlled loading.
probes: Can every parameter be post-build?
How do you prevent wrong dataset loading?

question: How should model ownership and ARXML merge conflicts be governed in distributed development?
answer: Assign authoritative ownership by model package and semantic object type.
Use controlled imports, baselines and automated validation rather than unrestricted shared editing.
Define change-request and compatibility rules for interfaces, communication and data types.
Resolve semantic conflicts in the owning domain, not by choosing the XML that merges cleanly.
Keep supplier delivery versions reproducible.
probes: Who owns a system signal used by three ECUs?
How do you handle duplicate short names?

question: What validations should run before RTE or BSW generation?
answer: Schema and reference integrity checks.
Model semantic and multiplicity checks.
Port/interface/data-type compatibility checks.
Communication mapping completeness and uniqueness checks.
ECUC dependency and range checks.
Project rules for timing, variants, safety and naming.
probes: Why is schema validation insufficient?
Which checks belong in CI?

question: A late OEM ARXML update changes a safety-related signal scaling and cycle time. What is the correct response?
answer: Identify affected system signals, mappings, data types, COM configuration and application assumptions.
Regenerate only from a controlled baseline after semantic review.
Reassess timing, range, plausibility and E2E behavior.
Repeat impacted unit, integration, network and safety tests.
Update traceability and supplier acceptance evidence.
probes: Can the change be handled only in COM?
What if the application expects the old physical range?

question: Design a reproducible AUTOSAR generation pipeline for a multi-supplier ECU.
answer: Pin schemas, vendor tools, plugins, licenses and generator versions.
Validate all incoming ARXML and record checksums/baselines.
Generate RTE and BSW in deterministic stages with logged inputs and outputs.
Run static checks, compilation, configuration consistency and regression tests.
Archive generated artifacts, reports and known deviations with the release.
probes: Should generated code be committed?
How do you detect nondeterministic output?

question: Describe the complete AUTOSAR workflow for adding a new brake-pressure signal from a sensor ECU to the ESC application.
answer: Define the physical data type and SWC port/interface contract.
Create system signal and communication mappings into I-PDU and CAN frame.
Allocate sender/receiver SWCs and update ECU Extracts.
Configure COM, PduR, CanIf and RTE on affected ECUs.
Add timeout, invalidation, E2E and plausibility behavior as required.
Generate, integrate and verify timing, scaling, faults and vehicle behavior.
probes: Which teams must approve the change?
Where is end-to-end latency verified?
